intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Astracantha qatmensis. However, I found that Astragalus spp. (which may be what you are referring to), also known as Astracantha, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family. 

Astragalus is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, primarily in Asia and Eastern Europe. It is known for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and enhancing cardiovascular health. Bioactive constituents of Astragalus include saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. 

Astragalus is often used in various herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and tinctures.
